name: Parsnip Chowder with Squash
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Butternut squash or sweet potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the parsnips and squash (or sweet potatoes) and cut each into 1/4-inch dice.
2. Cut the bacon into 1/4-inch strips and render it in a large saucepan over medium heat until lightly browned.
3. Transfer the bacon pieces to paper towels using a slotted spoon; discard all but 3 tablespoons of the rendered fat.
4. Add the onion and celery to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes until soft but not browned.
5. Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux.
6. Gradually whisk in the chicken stock, bringing the mixture to a boil; it will thicken slightly.
7. Add the bouquet garni, cinnamon stick, parsnip and squash dices, and bring back to a simmer.
8. Simmer the chowder for 6 to 8 minutes until the vegetables are tender but not soft.
9. Stir in the heavy cream, salt, pepper, cayenne, and nutmeg.
10. Just before serving, remove the bouquet garni and cinnamon stick; ladle the chowder into bowls and sprinkle each with chopped chives and a pinch of nutmeg.
Serves 6 to 8.
